Form 1041

  
U.S. Income Tax Return for Estates and Trusts \'b7 Complete - All Lines, Schedule G, Tax & Payments

  

  Yellow = Line requires your input
   \'b7 Green = Overpayment / Refund (IRC \'a76402)
   \'b7 White = Filled / synced from ledger
  

  

  
  
  

  

  
  

  
Form 1041

  
Department of the Treasury
Internal Revenue Service

  
OMB No. 1545-0092
20

  

  

  
U.S. Income Tax Return for Estates and Trusts

  
For calendar year 20__ or fiscal year beginning ______ 20__, and ending ______ 20__

  Go to www.irs.gov/Form1041 for instructions and the latest information.

  

  
  

  

  

  A Type of entity (check applicable box):

    
    
  

    
  

    
    
  


  Name of estate or trust:

  
  

  

  B Number of Schedules K-1 attached: 

  C Date entity created: 

  D Employer identification number:

  

  E Nonexempt charitable and split-interest trusts, check box: 

  Name and title of fiduciary:

  

  Address:

  
  

  

  

  
  
Income

  
1

  

  Interest income
  
  

  

  
2a

  

  Total ordinary dividends
  
  

  

  
2b

  

  Qualified dividends (included in line 2a)
  
  

  

  
3

  

  Business income or (loss) - attach Schedule C. Net profit flows from Schedule C Line 31. IRC \'a7162 expenses reduce \'a761 gross income.
  
  

  

  
4

  

  Capital gain or (loss) - attach Schedule D (Form 1041)
  
  

  

  
5

  

  Rents, royalties, partnerships, other estates and trusts, etc. - attach Schedule E
  
  

  

  
6

  

  Farm income or (loss) - attach Schedule F
  
  

  

  
7

  

  Ordinary gain or (loss) - attach Form 4797
  
  

  

  
8

  

  Other income - financial asset recognition, property interest, other (state nature on statement)
  
  

  

  
9

  

  Total income. Add lines 1 through 8
  
  

  

  
  
Deductions

  
10

  

  Interest - see instructions for deductible amount
  
  

  

  
11

  

  Taxes
  
  

  

  
12

  

  Fiduciary fees
  
  

  

  
13

  

  Charitable deduction - from Schedule A, line 7
  
  

  

  
14

  

  Attorney, accountant, and return preparer fees
  
  

  

  
15a

  

  Other deductions NOT subject to the 2% floor - attach schedule
  
  

  

  
15b

  

  Allowable miscellaneous itemized deductions subject to the 2% floor
  
  

  

  
16

  

  Total deductions. Add lines 10 through 15b
  
  

  

  
17

  

  Adjusted total income or (loss). Subtract line 16 from line 9
  
  

  

  
18

  

  Income distribution deduction - from Schedule B, line 15. Attach Schedules K-1
  
  

  

  
19

  

  Estate tax deduction including certain generation-skipping taxes - attach computation
  
  

  

  
20

  

  Net operating loss deduction - see instructions - IRC \'a7172
  
  

  

  
21

  

  Exemption: $600 (estate); $300 (simple trust); $100 (other trust). See instructions
  
  

  

  
22

  

  Add lines 18 through 21
  
  

  

  
23

  

  Taxable income. Subtract line 22 from line 17. If a loss, see instructions
  
  

  

  
  
Schedule G - Tax Computation (see instructions)

  
1

  

  Tax on taxable income from Tax Rate Schedule or check if:  Form 8814  Form 4972. Enter applicable tax
  
  

  

  
2a

  

  Foreign tax credit - attach Form 1116
  
  

  

  
2b

  

  General business credit - attach Form 3800 - IRC \'a738 - dollar-for-dollar reduction
  
  

  

  
2c

  

  Credit for prior year minimum tax - attach Form 8801
  
  

  

  
2d

  

  Bond credits from Form 8912
  
  

  

  
3

  

  Total credits. Add lines 2a through 2d
  
  

  

  
4

  

  Subtract line 3 from line 1 (if zero or less, enter -0-)
  
  

  

  
5

  

  Recapture taxes - check if from:  Form 4255  Form 8611  Form 8697  Form 8866
  
  

  

  
6

  

  Household employment taxes - attach Schedule H
  
  

  

  
7

  

  Total tax. Add lines 4 through 6. Enter here and on line 24
  
  

  

  
  
Tax and Payments

  
24

  

  Total tax - from Schedule G, line 7 (or include tax from Form 4972)
  
  

  

  
25

  

  Alternative minimum tax - attach Form 8082
  
  

  

  
26

  

  Net investment income tax - attach Form 8960 - IRC \'a71411
  
  

  

  
27

  

  Total tax. Add lines 24 through 26
  
  

  

  
  
Payments

  
28a

  

  2023 overpayment credited to 2024
  
  

  

  
28b

  

  2024 estimated tax payments and amount applied from 2023 return - IRC \'a76655
  
  

  

  
28c

  

  Estimated tax payments allocated to beneficiaries - from Form 1041-T
  
  

  

  
28d

  

  Subtract line 28c from the total of lines 28a and 28b
  
  

  

  
28e

  

  Tax paid with extension of time to file (Form 7004)
  
  

  

  
28f

  

  Federal income tax withheld - if any is from Form 1099, check here 
  
  

  

  
28g

  

  Other payments - Form 2439:  \'b7 Form 4136:  \'b7 Total 
  
  

  

  
29

  

  Total payments. Add lines 28d through 28g
  
  

  

  
30

  

  Estimated tax penalty - see instructions - check if Form 2210 attached 
  
  

  

  
31

  

  Tax due. If line 27 is larger than line 29, enter amount owed. See instructions
  
  

  

  
32

  

  Overpayment. If line 29 is larger than the total of lines 27 and 30, enter amount overpaid

  IRC \'a76402(a): Secretary SHALL credit or refund overpayment - mandatory, no discretion
  
  

  

  
33

  

  Amount of line 32 to be:
  Credited to 2025 estimated tax 
   Refunded  
  
  

  

  
  
Other Information

  

  

  1. Did the estate or trust receive tax-exempt income?  Yes  No

  2. Did the estate or trust receive all or any part of the earnings (salary, wages, and other compensation) of any individual by reason of a contract assignment or similar arrangement?  Yes  No

  3. At any time during calendar year 2024, did the estate or trust have an interest in or a signature or other authority over a bank, securities, or other financial account in a foreign country?  Yes  No

  If Yes, enter name of foreign country 
  

  

  4. During the tax year, did the estate or trust receive a distribution from, or was it the grantor of, or transferor to, a foreign trust?  Yes  No

  5. Did the estate or trust receive, or pay, any seller-financed mortgage interest?  Yes  No

  6. If this is an estate or complex trust making the section 663(b) election, check here 

  7. To make a section 643(e)(3) election, attach Schedule D and check here 

  8. If the decedent's estate has been open for more than 2 years, attach an explanation and check here 
  

  

  
  

  

  
Sign Here

  
Under penalties of perjury, I declare that I have examined this return, and accompanying schedules and statements, and to the best of my knowledge and belief, they are true, correct, and complete.

  
Fiduciary's Signature __________________________________________ Date ___________

  

  

  
Paid Preparer Use Only

  
Preparer's name: 

  
Preparer's signature: __________________________ Date: __________ PTIN: 

  
Firm name:  EIN: 

  
Phone: 

  
Check if self-employed: 

  

  

  
Cat. No. 11370H    Form 1041 (2024)

  

  

  

  
Form 3800

  
General Business Credit \'b7 Complete - Part I, Part II, Part III Carryforward Schedule \'b7 Flows to Form 1120 Schedule J Line 5c

  

  Yellow = Line requires your input
   \'b7 Green = Credit flows to Schedule J Line 5c (IRC \'a738)
   \'b7 White = Filled / synced from ledger
  

  

  
  
  

  

  
  

  
Form 3800

  
Department of the Treasury
Internal Revenue Service

  
OMB No. 1545-0895
20

  

  

  
General Business Credit

  
 Attach to your tax return.

  Go to www.irs.gov/Form3800 for instructions and the latest information.

  

  

  Name: 
     Identifying number (EIN): 
  

  
  
Part I - Current Year Credit for Credits Not Allowed Against Tentative Minimum Tax (TMT) - See instructions and complete Part(s) III before Part I

  
Complete a separate Part III for each source of credits listed in Part I. Use Part II to figure the amount of credits that can be used to reduce your tax.

  
1

  

  General business credits from partnerships, S corporations, estates, and trusts - from Schedule K-1
  
  

  

  
2

  

  Passive activity credits from partnerships, S corporations, estates, and trusts - from Schedule K-1
  
  

  

  
3

  

  Carryforward from current year activities and carryback from 2025 - from Part III, line 2. See instructions
  
  

  

  
4

  

  Passive activity credits allowed for current year - from Form 8582-CR or 8810. See instructions
  
  

  

  
5

  

  Add lines 1 through 4
  
  

  

  
  
Part II - Allowable Credit

  
6

  

  Regular tax before credits - see instructions
  
  

  

  
7

  

  Alternative minimum tax - from Form 4626 (corporations) or Form 6251 (individuals)
  
  

  

  
8

  

  Net income tax - add lines 6 and 7
  
  

  

  
9

  

  Net regular tax - see instructions
  
  

  

  
10

  

  Enter 25% (.25) of the excess, if any, of line 9 over $25,000 - see instructions
  
  

  

  
11

  

  Tentative minimum tax - see instructions
  
  

  

  
12

  

  Enter the greater of line 10 or line 11
  
  

  

  
13

  

  Subtract line 12 from line 8 - if zero or less, enter -0-
  
  

  

  
14

  

  Carryforward of general business credit to 2024 (from 2023 Form 3800, Part II, line 39) - IRC \'a739
  
  

  

  
15

  

  Carryback of general business credit from 2025 - see instructions
  
  

  

  
16

  

  Add lines 5, 14, and 15
  
  

  

  
17

  

  Allowable credit. Enter the smaller of line 13 or line 16. See instructions - dollar-for-dollar reduction of income tax - IRC \'a738

  This amount flows to: Form 1120 Schedule J Line 5c \'b7 Form 1041 Schedule G Line 2b \'b7 IRC \'a738 authorizes \'b7 IRC \'a739 allows carryforward
  
  

  

  
  
Part III - General Business Credits or Eligible Small Business Credits - Current Year Credit, Carryback, and Carryforward

  

  Complete a separate Part III for each type of credit included in line 4 above. Report amounts for the current year credit on line 1 and amounts for carryforwards and carrybacks on line 2.
  

  
  

  
(a)
Tax Yr

  
(b)
Form No.

  
(c) Description of Credit

  
(d)
Current
Year Credit

  
(e)
Prior Year
Carryforward

  
(f)
Carryback
from Future Yr

  
(g)
Total Available
(d)+(e)+(f)

  
(h)
Amount
Used

  

  
  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  
  
Part III Summary

  
18

  

  Current year credit. Add amounts in column (d), line 1 of all Part IIIs
  
  

  

  
19

  

  Carryforward to 2024 from 2023 Form 3800, Part II, line 39 - IRC \'a739 - carryforward 20 years
  
  

  

  
20

  

  Carryback from 2025 - see instructions for election to forgo carryback
  
  

  

  
21

  

  Add lines 18, 19, and 20 - total current year general business credit available
  
  

  

  
22

  

  Amount used to offset tax liability - from Part II, line 17 - IRC \'a738 dollar-for-dollar credit
  
  

  

  
38

  

  Total current year general business credits - add all column (d) amounts. This is the total credit amount before application against tax.

  Flow path: Form 3800 Line 38 Form 1120 Schedule J Part I Line 5c dollar-for-dollar reduction of income tax - IRC \'a738
  
  

  

  
39

  

  Carryforward to 2025 - subtract line 22 from line 21. Carry this unused credit forward up to 20 years - IRC \'a739
  
  

  

  
40

  

  Carryback to 2023 - any unused credit may be carried back 1 year - IRC \'a739(a) - attach amended return
  
  

  

  

  IRC \'a738: Allows credit against income tax for general business credits. IRC \'a739: Carryback 1 year / carryforward 20 years for unused credits.
  Flow: Form 3800 Line 38 (or Line 17) Form 1120 Schedule J Part I Line 5c reduces gross income tax dollar-for-dollar.
  If total estimated payments (IRC \'a76655) exceed net tax after Form 3800 credit overpayment Form 1120 Line 36/37 IRC \'a76402 mandates Secretary SHALL refund.
  

  
Cat. No. 12392F    Form 3800 (2024)

  

  

  

  
Form 1065

  
U.S. Return of Partnership Income \'b7 Complete - Pages 1\'965 \'b7 Schedule B \'b7 Schedule K \'b7 Schedule L \'b7 Schedule M-1 \'b7 Schedule M-2

  

  Yellow = Line requires your input
   \'b7 Green = Computed / distributable total
   \'b7 White = Filled
   \'b7  Gold text = reason this line must be completed
  

  

  
  
  

  

  
  

  
Form 1065

  
Department of the Treasury
Internal Revenue Service

  
OMB No. 1545-0123
20

  

  

  
U.S. Return of Partnership Income

  
For calendar year 2024, or tax year beginning _______ , 2024, ending _______ , 20___

  Go to www.irs.gov/Form1065 for instructions and the latest information.

  

  
  

  

  A Principal business activity: 
   Describe the partnership's primary business. Used for IRS statistical classification. IRC \'a76031 requires partnerships with income/loss to file Form 1065.

  B Principal product or service: 
   Describe the primary product or service offered. Helps IRS identify industry code for compliance purposes.

  C Business code no.: 
   6-digit NAICS code from IRS instructions. Required for correct industry classification on the return.

  D Employer identification number (EIN): 
   The partnership's own EIN - separate from any partner's TIN. Required on every partnership return under IRC \'a76109. This EIN is what the K-1 references in Part I, Line A.
  

  

  E Date business started: 

  F Total assets (see instructions): 
   Total assets from Schedule L, Line 14, Column (d). Required on Page 1. Used to determine Schedule M-3 filing requirement (total assets $10M).

  G Check applicable boxes:

    
    
  

    
  

  H Check accounting method:       
   EEON Foundation uses accrual basis - revenue recognized when earned, expense when incurred. IRC \'a7446 - taxpayer must use method that clearly reflects income. Accrual method is consistent with Glenshaw Glass (348 U.S. 426) asset recognition.

  I Number of Schedules K-1 attached: 
   One K-1 must be issued to each partner. IRC \'a76031(b) - partnership must furnish K-1 to each partner by the due date of the partnership return.
  

  

  

  Name of partnership: 
    Address: 
   Legal name and address of the partnership as registered with the IRS. Must match EIN records. IRC \'a76109.
  

  
  
Income

  

  
1a

  

  Gross receipts or sales
  
 Total gross receipts or sales from all business activities. IRC \'a761 - all income from whatever source derived. Starting point for computing partnership gross income.

  

  

  

  
1b

  

  Returns and allowances
  
 Subtract returns, refunds, and allowances given. Reduces line 1a to arrive at net sales on Line 1c. IRC \'a761.

  

  

  

  
1c

  

  Balance. Subtract line 1b from line 1a
  
 Net sales. Auto-computed: Line 1a minus Line 1b.

  

  

  

  
2

  

  Cost of goods sold (attach Form 1125-A)
  
 Direct cost of producing goods sold. Requires Form 1125-A if the partnership sells goods. IRC \'a7263A UNICAP rules may apply to inventory. Reduces gross profit.

  

  

  

  
3

  

  Gross profit. Subtract line 2 from line 1c
  
 Net revenue after cost of goods sold. Auto-computed.

  

  

  

  
4

  

  Ordinary income (loss) from other partnerships, estates, trusts (attach statement)
  
 Pass-through income from other entities in which this partnership holds an interest. IRC \'a7702 - each entity passes through its own items. Attach K-1s from those entities.

  

  

  

  
5

  

  Net farm profit (loss) (attach Schedule F)
  
 Net farming income or loss. Attach Schedule F. IRC \'a71402 - farming income is subject to self-employment tax for general partners.

  

  

  

  
6

  

  Net gain (loss) from Form 4797 Part II, line 17
  
 Gain or loss from sale of business property. IRC \'a71231 property. Attach Form 4797. Net Section 1231 gains are capital gains; net losses are ordinary losses.

  

  

  

  
7

  

  Other income (loss) - attach statement
  
 All other income not listed on Lines 1-6. Examples: cancellation of debt (IRC \'a7108), financial asset recognition (Glenshaw Glass), foreign currency gains. Attach itemized statement.

  

  

  

  
8

  

  Total income (loss). Combine lines 3 through 7
  
 Sum of all partnership income items. This is total gross income before deductions. Carried to Schedule K Line 1 for allocation to partners.

  

  

  
Deductions

  

  
9

  

  Salaries and wages (other than to partners, see instructions)
  
 Wages paid to non-partner employees. Deductible under IRC \'a7162(a)(1) as ordinary and necessary business expense. Do not include guaranteed payments to partners here (those go on Line 10).

  

  

  

  
10

  

  Guaranteed payments to partners (see instructions)
  
 Payments made to partners regardless of partnership income. IRC \'a7707(c) - treated as a partnership deduction and ordinary income to the recipient partner. Enter on Line 10 and pass through on Schedule K Line 4.

  

  

  

  
11

  

  Repairs and maintenance
  
 Ordinary repairs maintaining property in operating condition. Deductible under IRC \'a7162. Capital improvements are not deductible here - capitalize on Form 4562.

  

  

  

  
12

  

  Bad debts
  
 Uncollectible business receivables written off. IRC \'a7166 - business bad debts are deductible as ordinary losses. Must be bona fide debt that became worthless during the tax year.

  

  

  

  
13

  

  Rent
  
 Rent paid for property used in the partnership's trade or business. Deductible under IRC \'a7162(a)(3). Do not include rent paid to a partner - that is handled separately under IRC \'a7707(a).

  

  

  

  
14

  

  Taxes and licenses
  
 State/local taxes, payroll taxes, and business licenses. Deductible under IRC \'a7164. Federal income tax is NOT deductible here. Self-employment taxes paid by partners are deducted on their own returns.

  

  

  

  
15

  

  Interest (see instructions)
  
 Business interest expense. IRC \'a7163. Subject to IRC \'a7163(j) business interest limitation for certain taxpayers. Investment interest from portfolio income is separately reported on Schedule K Line 13b.

  

  

  

  
16a

  

  Depreciation (if required, attach Form 4562)
  
 Depreciation of business property under IRC \'a7167 and \'a7168. MACRS depreciation. IRC \'a7179 immediate expensing is separately reported on Schedule K Line 12. Attach Form 4562 if required.

  

  

  

  
16b

  

  Less: depreciation reported elsewhere on return
  
 Amount already included on Form 1125-A (cost of goods sold). Subtract to avoid double-counting depreciation already deducted against gross income.

  

  

  

  
16c

  

  Net depreciation. Subtract 16b from 16a
  
 Net depreciation deduction. Auto-computed.

  

  

  

  
17

  

  Depletion (do not deduct oil and gas depletion)
  
 Cost depletion for natural resources. IRC \'a7611. Oil and gas depletion is separately passed through to partners on Schedule K Line 17 so each partner can compute their own percentage depletion under IRC \'a7613.

  

  

  

  
18

  

  Retirement plans, etc.
  
 Partnership contributions to qualified retirement plans for non-partner employees. IRC \'a7404. Partner retirement plan contributions are deducted on the partner's own return, not here.

  

  

  

  
19

  

  Employee benefit programs
  
 Health insurance, group-term life, and other employee benefits for non-partner employees. Deductible under IRC \'a7162. Benefits provided to partners are governed by IRC \'a7707(c) or \'a71372.

  

  

  

  
20

  

  Other deductions (attach statement)
  
 All other ordinary and necessary business deductions under IRC \'a7162 not listed on Lines 9-19. Must attach a detailed schedule itemizing each deduction with amount and IRC authority.

  

  

  

  
21

  

  Total deductions. Add lines 9 through 20
  
 Sum of all deductions. Subtracted from Line 8 Total Income to arrive at ordinary business income on Line 22.

  

  

  

  
22

  

  Ordinary business income (loss). Subtract line 21 from line 8
  
 Net ordinary business income or loss of the partnership. This is the figure that flows to Schedule K Line 1 and then to each partner's K-1 Line 1 in proportion to their distributive share. IRC \'a7702.

  

  

  
  
Schedule B - Other Information

  

  1. What type of entity is filing this return?

    
    
  

    
    
   

   Entity type determines the tax rules, self-employment treatment of partners, and state filing requirements. IRC \'a7761 defines a partnership for federal tax purposes.
  2. At any time during the tax year, was any partner in the partnership a disregarded entity, a partnership (including an entity treated as a partnership), a trust, an S corporation, an estate, or a nominee or similar person?  Yes    No
   If yes, special reporting rules apply for tiered partnerships. The IRS requires disclosure to ensure income is properly reported at each tier. IRC \'a7704(e) - family partnership rules.
  3. At the end of the tax year:

    a. Did any foreign or domestic corporation, partnership (including any entity treated as a partnership), trust, or tax-exempt organization, or any foreign government own, directly or indirectly, an interest of 50% or more in the profit, loss, or capital of the partnership?
   Yes    No

   Required disclosure under IRC \'a76038 and \'a76038A. Majority ownership by tax-exempt entities may affect the partnership's tax status and reporting obligations.
  4. Does the partnership satisfy all four of the following conditions?

  (b) The partnership's total assets at the end of the tax year were less than $1 million;

  (d) The partnership is not filing, and is not required to file, Schedule M-3.
   Yes    No
   If all four conditions are met, the partnership is not required to complete Schedules L, M-1, and M-2. However, completing these schedules creates a complete evidentiary record of financial position consistent with accrual accounting practice.
  5. Is this partnership a publicly traded partnership as defined in IRC \'a7469(k)(2)?
   Yes    No
   Publicly traded partnerships are subject to special passive activity rules under IRC \'a7469(k). Losses from PTPs can only offset income from the same PTP.
  6. During the tax year, did the partnership have any debt that was cancelled, was forgiven, or had the terms modified so as to reduce the principal amount of the debt?
   Yes    No
   Cancellation of debt is generally includible in income under IRC \'a7108. Exceptions apply for insolvency, qualified farm debt, and qualified business real property. If yes, each partner may need Form 982.
  7. Has this partnership filed, or is it required to file, Form 8918 (Material Advisor Disclosure Statement) to provide information on any reportable transaction?
   Yes    No

   Reportable transactions include listed transactions, transactions of interest, and certain loss transactions. IRC \'a76011 and \'a76111 disclosure requirements. Failure to disclose carries penalties under IRC \'a76707A.
  8. At any time during calendar year 2024, did the partnership have an interest in or a signature or other authority over a financial account in a foreign country?
   Yes    No
   Foreign financial account interests may require filing FinCEN Form 114 (FBAR) and/or Form 8938 (FATCA). Failure to file carries severe civil and criminal penalties. Bank Secrecy Act \'a75314.
  9. Is the partnership electing out of the centralized partnership audit regime under IRC \'a76221(b)?
   Yes    No
   Small partnerships (100 or fewer eligible partners, all eligible partners are individuals, C corporations, S corporations, or estates of deceased partners) may elect out. Election out means the IRS audits partners individually rather than at the partnership level. IRC \'a76221(b).
  

  
  
Schedule K - Partners' Distributive Share Items - Total amounts distributed to all partners

  

  These totals are the partnership-level amounts. They are then divided among partners on their individual K-1s based on each partner's percentage interest (IRC \'a7704).
  Line 1 should equal Form 1065 Page 1, Line 22.
  

  
Income (Loss)

  

  
1

  

  Ordinary business income (loss) - from page 1, line 22
  
 Net ordinary business income carried directly from Page 1, Line 22. This is the primary income item passed through to partners on their K-1 Line 1. IRC \'a7702(a)(8).

  

  

  

  
2

  

  Net rental real estate income (loss) - attach Form 8825
  
 Rental real estate income or loss. Separately stated because it is subject to passive activity rules (IRC \'a7469). Attach Form 8825 for each rental property. Flows to each partner's K-1 Line 2.

  

  

  

  
3

  

  Other net rental income (loss) - attach statement
  
 Rental income other than real estate. Separately stated for IRC \'a7469 passive activity application. Flows to K-1 Line 3.

  

  

  

  
4

  

  Guaranteed payments: (a) Services
  
 Total guaranteed payments to all partners for services. Deducted on Page 1 Line 10. Each partner's share shown on their K-1 Line 4. IRC \'a7707(c).

  

  

  

  
5

  

  Interest income - IRC \'a761(a)(4)
  
 Total partnership interest income. Separately stated because partners may qualify for different treatment (e.g., tax-exempt partners). Flows to K-1 Line 5.

  

  

  

  
6a

  

  Ordinary dividends
  
 Total ordinary dividends. Separately stated so partners can apply their own DRD or foreign tax credit rules. IRC \'a761(a)(7). Flows to K-1 Line 6a.

  

  

  

  
6b

  

  Qualified dividends
  
 Portion of ordinary dividends qualifying for preferential rates under IRC \'a71(h)(11). Included within Line 6a. Flows to K-1 Line 6b.

  

  

  

  
7

  

  Royalties - IRC \'a761(a)(6)
  
 Total royalty income. Separately stated for partner-level treatment. IRC \'a761(a)(6). Flows to K-1 Line 7.

  

  

  

  
8

  

  Net short-term capital gain (loss) - IRC \'a71222
  
 Net short-term capital gain/loss. Separately stated so partners can net with their own short-term transactions on Schedule D. IRC \'a71222(5). Flows to K-1 Line 8.

  

  

  

  
9a

  

  Net long-term capital gain (loss) - IRC \'a71222
  
 Net long-term capital gain/loss. Taxed at preferential rates under IRC \'a71(h). Separately stated so partners can apply their own rate calculations. Flows to K-1 Line 9a.

  

  

  

  
9b

  

  Collectibles (28%) gain (loss)
  
 Gain/loss from collectibles. Taxed at maximum 28% rate under IRC \'a71(h)(5). Separately stated for partner-level rate computation. Flows to K-1 Line 9b.

  

  

  

  
9c

  

  Unrecaptured section 1250 gain
  
 Depreciation recapture on real property. Taxed at maximum 25% rate under IRC \'a71(h)(6). Must be separately stated so each partner can compute their own 25% rate gain on Schedule D. Flows to K-1 Line 9c.

  

  

  

  
10

  

  Net section 1231 gain (loss) - IRC \'a71231
  
 Net gain/loss from sale of IRC \'a71231 property (trade/business property held > 1 year). Net gain = long-term capital gain; net loss = ordinary loss. Must be separately stated. Flows to K-1 Line 10.

  

  

  

  
11

  

  Other income (loss) - attach statement
  
 All other income items not listed above. Examples: Section 988 currency gain, Section 1256 contracts, cancellation of debt income (IRC \'a7108). Separately stated for proper partner treatment. Flows to K-1 Line 11.

  

  

  
Deductions

  

  
12

  

  Section 179 deduction - IRC \'a7179
  
 Immediate expensing election passed through to partners. Each partner applies their own income limitation and at-risk limitation at their level. IRC \'a7179(d)(8) - partners must reduce their own basis. Flows to K-1 Line 12.

  

  

  

  
13a

  

  Cash contributions (60%)
  
 Cash charitable contributions subject to 60% AGI limitation. Separately stated so each partner applies their own AGI limitation. IRC \'a7170(b)(1)(A). Flows to K-1 Line 13a.

  

  

  

  
13b

  

  Cash contributions (30%)
  
 Cash contributions to 30%-limit organizations (veterans, fraternal societies, certain private foundations). IRC \'a7170(b)(1)(B). Subject to lower 30% AGI cap. Flows to K-1 Line 13b.

  

  

  

  
13c

  

  Noncash contributions (50%)
  
 Fair market value of donated property subject to 50% AGI limitation. Requires Form 8283 for donations over $500. IRC \'a7170(b). Flows to K-1 Line 13c.

  

  

  

  
14

  

  Self-employment earnings (loss) - IRC \'a71402
  
 Net earnings from self-employment passed through to general partners. IRC \'a71402(a) - distributive share of net earnings subject to SE tax. Flows to K-1 Line 14. Partners compute SE tax on Schedule SE.

  

  

  
Credits

  

  
15

  

  Low-income housing credit (IRC \'a742) - attach Form 8586
  
 IRC \'a742 low-income housing tax credit passed through to partners. Dollar-for-dollar credit against income tax. Attach Form 8586. Flows to K-1 Line 15a. Partners report on their Form 3800.

  

  

  

  
15b

  

  Other rental real estate credits - attach statement
  
 Other credits from rental real estate activities. Separately stated for passive activity credit rules under IRC \'a7469. Flows to K-1 Line 15b.

  

  

  

  
15c

  

  Other rental credits - attach statement
  
 Credits from other rental activities. IRC \'a7469 passive activity rules apply at the partner level. Flows to K-1 Line 15c.

  

  

  

  
15d

  

  Other credits - attach statement - IRC \'a738
  
 General business credits and other credits passed through to partners. IRC \'a738 - each partner claims the credit on their own Form 3800. Dollar-for-dollar reduction of partner's income tax. Flows to K-1 Line 15.

  

  

  
Foreign Transactions

  

  
16a

  

  Name of country or U.S. possession
  
 Required if partnership has foreign source income subject to foreign tax credit under IRC \'a7901. Partners need this to compute their own foreign tax credit limitations under IRC \'a7904. Flows to Schedule K-3 and K-1 Line 16.

  

  

  

  
16b

  

  Gross income from all sources
  
 Total gross income from all sources - used in foreign tax credit limitation computation. IRC \'a7904(d) - basket rules apply. Partners need this for Form 1116.

  

  

  
Alternative Minimum Tax (AMT) Items

  

  
17a

  

  Post-1986 depreciation adjustment
  
 Difference between regular MACRS depreciation (Line 16) and AMT depreciation (ADS method). IRC \'a756(a)(1). Separately stated so each partner adds this to their own AMT computation on Form 6251.

  

  

  

  
17b

  

  Adjusted gain or loss
  
 Difference between regular and AMT gain/loss on asset dispositions. IRC \'a756(a)(6). Flows to K-1 Line 17b for partner's AMT computation.

  

  

  
Other Information

  

  
18a

  

  Tax-exempt interest income - IRC \'a7103
  
 Tax-exempt interest from municipal bonds. IRC \'a7103 - not includible in gross income but reported here because it increases partner basis (IRC \'a7705(a)(1)(B)) and may affect other calculations.

  

  

  

  
18b

  

  Other tax-exempt income
  
 Other income exempt from federal tax. Like tax-exempt interest, this increases partner basis without being taxable income. Must be disclosed to partners.

  

  

  

  
18c

  

  Nondeductible expenses
  
 Expenses that are not deductible under the IRC (e.g., fines, political contributions, IRC \'a7274 disallowed meals). Reduces partner basis under IRC \'a7705(a)(2)(B) but is not a deduction.

  

  

  

  
19a

  

  Cash and marketable securities distributions
  
 Cash distributions to partners. IRC \'a7731 - generally not taxable to extent of outside basis. Excess = capital gain. Reduces each partner's outside basis. Must be reported on K-1 Line 19.

  

  

  

  
19b

  

  Distribution of other property
  
 Non-cash property distributions. IRC \'a7731 - generally no gain recognized at partnership level. Partner takes partnership's adjusted basis in distributed property. IRC \'a7732.

  

  

  

  
20a

  

  Investment income
  
 Gross investment income (interest, dividends, royalties, etc.) used in investment interest expense limitation under IRC \'a7163(d). Partners need this to compute their own investment interest limitation.

  

  

  

  
20b

  

  Investment expenses
  
 Investment expenses directly connected to investment income. IRC \'a7163(d) - reduces net investment income for investment interest expense deduction limitation at partner level.

  

  

  
  
Schedule L - Balance Sheet per Books (Beginning and End of Tax Year)

  

  

  
Assets

  
(a) Beg of Year

  
(b) End of Year

  
Liabilities & Capital

  

  

  

  
1

  
Cash
 Cash on hand and in bank accounts. IRC \'a7446 - accrual basis requires recording cash as a balance sheet item. Beginning and ending balances must reconcile to Schedule M-1.

  

  

  

  

  
2a

  
Trade notes and accounts receivable
 Amounts owed to the partnership from customers. Accrual basis - revenue recognized when earned (Glenshaw Glass). Must be recorded even if not yet collected.

  

  

  

  

  
2b

  
Less: allowance for bad debts
 Reserve for uncollectible receivables. IRC \'a7166 - reduces net receivables to net realizable value. Enter as negative number.

  

  

  

  

  
3

  
Inventories
 Inventory held for sale. IRC \'a7471 inventory rules. UNICAP rules may require capitalization of certain costs under IRC \'a7263A.

  

  

  

  

  
4

  
U.S. government obligations
 Treasury bills, notes, bonds. Interest is exempt from state tax but subject to federal income tax. Report interest separately on Schedule K Line 5.

  

  

  

  

  
5

  
Tax-exempt securities - IRC \'a7103
 Municipal bonds and other tax-exempt obligations. IRC \'a7103 interest is not gross income but affects partner basis and investment interest limitation. Report on Schedule K Line 18a.

  

  

  

  

  
6

  
Other current assets (attach statement)
 Other short-term assets including prepaid expenses, deposits, and short-term investments. Attach schedule identifying each item.

  

  

  

  

  
7a

  
Loans to partners (or persons related to partners)
 Loans made by the partnership to partners. IRC \'a7707(a) - treated as if made to an unrelated party. Must bear arm's-length interest under IRC \'a77872 to avoid below-market loan income imputation.

  

  

  

  

  
7b

  
Mortgage and real estate loans
 Real estate mortgage loans held as assets. Report interest income on Schedule K Line 5. Value at outstanding principal balance.

  

  

  

  

  
8

  
Other investments (attach statement)
 Long-term investments in securities, other partnerships, and assets. Per Glenshaw Glass (348 U.S. 426), assets with ascertainable FMV are recordable financial assets. Attach schedule.

  

  

  

  

  
9a

  
Buildings and other depreciable assets
 Gross cost of depreciable property. IRC \'a7167/\'a7168 MACRS depreciation. Report depreciation on Form 4562 and Line 16 of Page 1.

  

  

  

  

  
9b

  
Less: accumulated depreciation
 Total depreciation taken to date. Reduces book value of assets. Enter as negative number. Used in Schedule M-1 reconciliation.

  

  

  

  

  
10a

  
Depletable assets
 Natural resource assets subject to depletion. IRC \'a7611. Oil and gas depletion computed at partner level - report separately on Schedule K.

  

  

  

  

  
11

  
Land (net of any amortization)
 Cost of land. Land is NOT depreciable under IRC \'a7168. Net of any Section 197 amortization if land rights are included.

  

  

  

  

  
12

  
Intangible assets (amortizable only)
 Goodwill, customer lists, patents, and other IRC \'a7197 intangibles. Amortized over 15 years. IRC \'a7197.

  

  

  

  

  
13

  
Other assets (attach statement)
 All other assets not listed above including refund receivables (IRC \'a76402 overpayments), NOL financial assets (IRC \'a7172), and other documented economic interests. Attach statement.

  

  

  

  

  
14

  
Total assets
 Sum of all assets. Must equal total liabilities plus partners' capital. This figure also goes on Page 1, Line F.

  

  

  

  
Liabilities and Capital

  

  
15

  
Accounts payable
 Amounts owed to vendors and suppliers. Accrual basis - liabilities recognized when incurred regardless of payment. IRC \'a7446.

  

  

  

  

  
16

  
Mortgages, notes, bonds payable in less than 1 year
 Current portion of long-term debt and all short-term borrowings. Affects partners' share of recourse liabilities on K-1 Part II Line K, which determines outside basis under IRC \'a7752.

  

  

  

  

  
17

  
Other current liabilities (attach statement)
 Accrued wages, accrued taxes, deferred revenue, and other short-term obligations. Attach schedule. Accrual basis requires these be recorded when incurred.

  

  

  

  

  
18

  
All nonrecourse loans
 Loans for which no partner bears personal liability. IRC \'a7752 - allocated among partners based on profit-sharing ratios. Nonrecourse liabilities included in partner's basis but cannot create deductible loss beyond at-risk amount.

  

  

  

  

  
19

  
Loans from partners (or persons related to partners)
 Amounts borrowed from partners. IRC \'a7707(a) - treated as third-party loans. Must bear arm's-length interest under IRC \'a77872. These are recourse to the lending partner.

  

  

  

  

  
20

  
Mortgages, notes, bonds payable in 1 year or more
 Long-term debt obligations. The partner's share of recourse vs. nonrecourse classification affects their outside basis (IRC \'a7752) and at-risk amount (IRC \'a7465).

  

  

  

  

  
21

  
Other liabilities (attach statement)
 All other liabilities not listed above. Include deferred tax liabilities, contingent liabilities, and other accrued obligations. Attach itemized schedule.

  

  

  

  

  
22

  
Partners' capital accounts
 Total of all partners' capital accounts. Must equal Total Assets (Line 14) minus Total Liabilities (Lines 15-21). Reconciles to Schedule M-2 ending capital. IRC \'a7704(b).

  

  

  

  
  
Schedule M-1 - Reconciliation of Income (Loss) per Books With Income (Loss) per Return

  

  Note: The partnership may be required to file Schedule M-3 instead (if total assets $10 million). Schedule M-3 is more detailed. Schedule M-1 is required for all partnerships not filing M-3.
  

  

  
1

  

  Net income (loss) per books
  
 Net income or loss per the partnership's financial books/accounting records. Starting point for reconciliation. Accrual basis book income per GAAP or IRC \'a7446 method. May differ from tax return income due to timing differences and permanent differences.

  

  

  

  
2

  

  Income included on Schedule K not recorded on books this year (itemize)
  
 Income taxable in the current year but not yet recorded in the books (e.g., prepaid income taxable when received under claim-of-right doctrine). Common book-tax difference. Must be added back to reconcile from book to tax income.

  

  

  

  
3

  

  Guaranteed payments (other than health insurance)
  
 Guaranteed payments deducted on Page 1 Line 10 but may be recorded differently in books. IRC \'a7707(c) - must be separately identified in reconciliation.

  

  

  

  
4

  

  Expenses recorded on books this year not included on Schedule K
  
 Book expenses not deductible for tax - examples: fines (IRC \'a7162(f)), political contributions, 50% disallowed meals (IRC \'a7274), premiums for life insurance where partnership is beneficiary (IRC \'a7264). These are permanent differences increasing taxable income above book income.

  

  

  

  
5

  

  Income recorded on books this year not included on Schedule K
  
 Tax-exempt income in books not included in partnership taxable income - primarily tax-exempt interest (IRC \'a7103) and life insurance proceeds (IRC \'a7101). Reduces taxable income below book income.

  

  

  

  
6

  

  Deductions on Schedule K not charged against book income
  
 Tax deductions not recorded as book expenses - examples: percentage depletion in excess of cost depletion (IRC \'a7613), accelerated depreciation over book depreciation, IRC \'a7179 expensing. These are book-tax timing/permanent differences reducing taxable income below book income.

  

  

  

  
7

  

  Income (loss) per Schedule K (line 8 minus lines 5 and 6, plus lines 2, 3, and 4)
  
 Tax return income reconciled from book income. This must equal Schedule K, Line 1 (ordinary business income). If Line 7 does not equal Schedule K Line 1, an error exists in the reconciliation that must be corrected before filing.

  

  

  
  
Schedule M-2 - Analysis of Partners' Capital Accounts

  

  Report the total of all partners' capital accounts. The ending balance must equal Schedule L, Line 22, Column (d). Each partner's individual capital account is reconciled on their K-1 Part II, Line L.
  

  

  
1

  

  Balance at beginning of year
  
 Total of all partners' capital accounts at the start of the tax year. Must equal Schedule L Line 22, Column (b) (beginning of year). Also equal to prior year Schedule M-2, Line 9 (ending balance). IRC \'a7704(b).

  

  

  

  
2

  

  Capital contributed: Cash
  
 Total cash contributed by all partners during the year. IRC \'a7721 - no gain or loss recognized on contribution of property to a partnership in exchange for a partnership interest. Increases partner outside basis by amount contributed.

  

  

  

  
2b

  

  Capital contributed: Property
  
 Fair market value (or agreed-upon book value) of non-cash property contributed by partners. IRC \'a7721 - no gain recognized. Partner's basis in contributed property carries over to the partnership (IRC \'a7723). Report any IRC \'a7704(c) built-in gain separately.

  

  

  

  
3

  

  Net income (loss) per books
  
 Current year net income or loss allocated to partners' capital accounts per the books. This is the amount that increases (income) or decreases (loss) each partner's capital interest. Should reconcile to Schedule M-1 Line 1.

  

  

  

  
4

  

  Other increases (itemize)
  
 Any other increases to partners' capital not listed above - examples: forgiveness of partner debt, revaluation of partnership assets (IRC \'a7704(b) "book-up"), restoration of deficit capital. Attach itemized statement.

  

  

  

  
5

  

  Add lines 1 through 4
  
 Total capital before distributions and decreases. Sum of beginning balance, contributions, net income, and other increases.

  

  

  

  
6

  

  Distributions: Cash
  
 Total cash distributed to all partners during the year. IRC \'a7731 - generally not taxable to extent of outside basis. Reduces each partner's outside basis. Excess over basis = capital gain recognized by the partner.

  

  

  

  
6b

  

  Distributions: Property
  
 Fair market value of non-cash property distributed to partners. IRC \'a7731/\'a7732 - no gain/loss at partnership level generally. Reduces partner basis by partnership's adjusted basis in distributed property.

  

  

  

  
7

  

  Other decreases (itemize)
  
 Any other decreases to capital not listed above - examples: write-offs, revaluation decreases, partner withdrawal agreements. Attach itemized statement.

  

  

  

  
8

  

  Add lines 6 and 7
  
 Total distributions and other decreases.

  

  

  

  
9

  

  Balance at end of year. Subtract line 8 from line 5
  
 Ending total partners' capital. Must equal Schedule L Line 22, Column (d) (end of year). This is the aggregate of all K-1 Part II, Line L ending capital balances. If there is a discrepancy, review all K-1 capital account computations. IRC \'a7704(b).

  

  

  
  

  

  
Sign Here - Under penalties of perjury, I declare that I have examined this return, including accompanying schedules and statements, and to the best of my knowledge and belief, it is true, correct, and complete.

  
Signature of general partner or limited liability company member manager _____________________________ Date ___________

  
Print/Type preparer's name ___________________________ Preparer's signature _________________________ Date ___________

  

  

  
Paid Preparer Use Only

  
Firm's name: 

  
Firm's EIN:  Phone: 

  
Firm's address: 

  

  IRC \'a76031: Every partnership must file an annual information return (Form 1065) reporting items of income, gain, loss, deduction, and credit, and issue a Schedule K-1 to each partner.
  IRC \'a76698: Failure to file penalty = $235 per partner per month (up to 12 months) for tax year 2024.
  

  

  

  
Cat. No. 11390Z    Form 1065 (2024)

  

  

  

  
Schedule K-1 (Form 1065)

  
Partner's Share of Income, Deductions, Credits, etc. \'b7 Complete - Parts I, II, III \'b7 Tax Year 2024

  

  Yellow = Line requires your input
   \'b7 White = Filled
   \'b7  Gold text below field = reason this line must be completed
  

  

  
  

  

  
  

  

  
Schedule K-1

  
(Form 1065)
Department of the Treasury
Internal Revenue Service

  

  
OMB No. 1545-0123
20
For calendar year 2024, or tax
year beginning _____ , 2024
ending ____________ , 20___

  

  

  
Partner's Share of Income, Deductions, Credits, etc.

  
 See separate instructions.

  

  
  

  
  

  
Part I - Information About the Partnership

  

  A Partnership's employer identification number (EIN):

  

  
   Required to identify the partnership filing Form 1065. IRS matches this EIN to the partnership return. IRC \'a76031 requires partnerships to file and issue K-1s to each partner.
  

  

  

  B Partnership's name, address, city, state, and ZIP:

  

  
  
  
   Full legal name and address of the partnership that issued this K-1. Must match the name on Form 1065. Required for IRS matching.
  

  

  

  C IRS center where partnership filed return:

  

  
   Enter the IRS service center where Form 1065 was filed. Required for return matching and audit trail.
  

  

  

  D Check if this is a publicly traded partnership (PTP): 
   PTPs have special passive activity rules under IRC \'a7469(k). Check only if the partnership trades on an established securities market.
  

  

  
  

  
Part II - Information About the Partner

  

  E Partner's SSN or TIN (do not use TIN of a disregarded entity):

  

  
   Partner's taxpayer identification number. Required by IRC \'a76722 - failure to provide correct TIN subjects partnership to penalty. Used by IRS to match partner's individual return.
  

  

  

  F Partner's name, address, city, state, and ZIP:

  

  
  
  
   Legal name and address of the partner (EEON Foundation). Must match the partner's own tax return. Used by IRS to associate the K-1 income/loss with the partner's return.
  

  

  

  G General partner or LLC member-manager:    Limited partner or other LLC member: 
   Check the applicable box. General partners/managing members are subject to self-employment tax on their distributive share. Limited partners generally are not. IRC \'a71402(a)(13).
  

  

  H1 Domestic partner:    Foreign partner: 
   Foreign partners are subject to withholding under IRC \'a71446. Domestic partners are not. This affects withholding obligations of the partnership.
  

  

  H2 If the partner is a disregarded entity (DE), enter the partner's:
  TIN 
  Type of entity 
   Required if EEON Foundation is a disregarded entity (e.g., single-member LLC). The IRS must know the underlying owner's TIN for proper assessment. IRC \'a77701.
  

  

  I1 What type of entity is this partner?
  
   Enter the type of entity. Different entities have different tax treatment of K-1 items. Corporations use Form 1120; trusts use Form 1041; individuals use Schedule E (Form 1040).
  

  

  I2 If the partner is a retirement plan (IRA/SEP/Keogh/etc.), check here: 
  

  

  J Partner's share of profit, loss, and capital (see instructions):
  

  
Beginning %
Ending %
Check if decrease due to sale/exchange

  
Profit

  

  

  

  
Loss

  

  

  

  
Capital

  

  

  

  

   Partner's percentage share of profit, loss, and capital. Required to allocate partnership income/loss items to this partner's return under IRC \'a7704. Beginning % = start of year; Ending % = end of year. Must total 100% across all partners.
  

  

  K Partner's share of liabilities:
  

  
Nonrecourse: 

  
Qualified nonrecourse financing: 

  
Recourse: 

  

   Partner's share of partnership liabilities determines basis under IRC \'a7752. Recourse liabilities are generally allocated to the partner at risk. Nonrecourse liabilities are allocated per the partnership agreement. Affects basis and at-risk rules IRC \'a7465.
  

  

  L Partner's capital account analysis:
  

  Beginning capital: 

  Capital contributed: 

  Current year net income/(loss): 

  Other increase/(decrease): 

  Withdrawals/distributions: 

  Ending capital: 
  

  

  Tax basis:    GAAP:    Section 704(b) book:    Other (explain): 
  

   Partner's capital account tracks the economic interest in the partnership. Required under IRC \'a7704(b) to properly allocate income and loss items. Ending capital + liabilities = partner's basis for determining gain/loss on disposition.
  

  

  

  
  
Part III - Partner's Share of Current Year Income, Deductions, Credits, and Other Items

  

  
  

  

  
Line

  
Item

  
Amount

  

  
  

  
1

  
Ordinary business income (loss) - IRC \'a7702(a)(8)

  

  
   Your share of the partnership's net ordinary business income or loss from Form 1065 Line 22. Flows to Schedule E (Form 1040) or Form 1120 Line 1/10. IRC \'a7702 - each partner takes their distributive share.
  

  

  

  
2

  
Net rental real estate income (loss) - IRC \'a7702

  

  
   Your share of rental real estate income/loss from Form 8825. Generally passive under IRC \'a7469. Flows to Schedule E. Subject to at-risk rules IRC \'a7465.
  

  

  

  
3

  
Other net rental income (loss)

  

  
   Your share of other rental income not from real estate. Report on Schedule E. IRC \'a7469 passive activity rules apply.
  

  

  

  
4

  
Guaranteed payments for services

  

  
   Payments guaranteed to the partner regardless of partnership income. Treated as ordinary income to recipient under IRC \'a7707(c). Deductible by partnership.
  

  

  

  
4b

  
Guaranteed payments for capital

  

  
   Guaranteed return on capital contribution treated as IRC \'a7707(c) income. Enter on Schedule E.
  

  

  

  
4c

  
Total guaranteed payments

  

  
   Sum of Lines 4 and 4b. Your total guaranteed payments - enter as ordinary income on your return.
  

  

  

  
5

  
Interest income - IRC \'a761(a)(4)

  

  
   Your distributive share of partnership interest income. Include on Form 1040 Schedule B or Form 1120 Line 5. IRC \'a761(a)(4) - interest is includible gross income.
  

  

  

  
6a

  
Ordinary dividends - IRC \'a761(a)(7)

  

  
   Your share of ordinary dividends. Report on Schedule B or Form 1120 Line 4. IRC \'a761(a)(7).
  

  

  

  
6b

  
Qualified dividends

  

  
   Qualified dividends taxed at preferential capital gains rates under IRC \'a71(h). Included within Line 6a - not in addition to it.
  

  

  

  
6c

  
Dividend equivalents

  

  
   Payments treated as dividends under IRC \'a7871(m) for financial instruments. Report as ordinary dividend income.
  

  

  

  
7

  
Royalties - IRC \'a761(a)(6)

  

  
   Your share of royalty income. IRC \'a761(a)(6) - royalties are gross income. Report on Schedule E or Form 1120.
  

  

  

  
8

  
Net short-term capital gain (loss) - IRC \'a71222(5)

  

  
   Your share of short-term capital gain/loss (assets held 1 year). Report on Schedule D. Taxed as ordinary income rates. IRC \'a71222(5).
  

  

  

  
9a

  
Net long-term capital gain (loss) - IRC \'a71222(7)

  

  
   Your share of long-term capital gain/loss (assets held > 1 year). Report on Schedule D. Subject to preferential rates under IRC \'a71(h). IRC \'a71222(7).
  

  

  

  
  

  

  
Line

  
Item

  
Amount

  

  

  
9b

  
Collectibles (28%) gain (loss)

  

  
   Gain/loss from collectibles taxed at maximum 28% rate. IRC \'a71(h)(5). Report on Schedule D Part II.
  

  

  

  
9c

  
Unrecaptured IRC \'a71250 gain

  

  
   Gain attributable to depreciation on real property - taxed at maximum 25% rate. IRC \'a71(h)(6). Report on Schedule D Unrecaptured \'a71250 worksheet.
  

  

  

  
10

  
Net section 1231 gain (loss) - IRC \'a71231

  

  
   Your share of Section 1231 gain/loss from trade/business property held > 1 year. Net gain = capital gain. Net loss = ordinary loss. IRC \'a71231 - Report on Form 4797.
  

  

  

  
11

  
Other income (loss) - attach statement

  

  
   Other income items not separately stated above. Examples: cancellation of debt income (IRC \'a7108), Section 1256 contracts, foreign currency gain. Attach detailed statement.
  

  

  

  
12

  
Section 179 deduction - IRC \'a7179

  

  
   Your share of the partnership's Section 179 immediate expensing election. Subject to your own taxable income limitation and at-risk rules. Carries to your own Form 4562. IRC \'a7179.
  

  

  

  
13

  
Other deductions - attach statement

  

  
   Deductions passed through to partners that are not separately stated elsewhere. Examples: IRC \'a759(e) mining costs, environmental cleanup costs. Attach itemized statement.
  

  

  

  
14

  
Self-employment earnings (loss) - IRC \'a71402

  

  
   Income subject to self-employment tax for general partners and active LLC members. IRC \'a71402(a) - net earnings from self-employment. Flows to Schedule SE.
  

  

  

  
15

  
Credits - attach statement - IRC \'a738

  

  
   Your share of general business credits from the partnership. IRC \'a738 - flows to your Form 3800. Dollar-for-dollar reduction of your income tax liability. Attach credit statement.
  

  

  

  
16

  
Schedule K-3 is attached if checked: see instructions

  

  
   If the partnership has foreign activities or transactions with foreign partners, Schedule K-3 reports your international tax items. IRC \'a7904 foreign tax credit limitation.
  

  

  

  
17

  
Alternative minimum tax (AMT) items - IRC \'a755

  

  
   Adjustments and tax preference items for Alternative Minimum Tax computation. Include on Form 6251. IRC \'a755-\'a758 AMT items passed through from partnership.
  

  

  

  
18

  
Tax-exempt income and nondeductible expenses

  

  
   Tax-exempt interest and income (IRC \'a7103) and nondeductible expenses reduce your basis but are not taxable. Affects partner's outside basis. IRC \'a7705(a)(2)(B).
  

  

  

  
19

  
Distributions - IRC \'a7731

  

  
   Cash and property distributions received from partnership. Generally not taxable to extent of outside basis (IRC \'a7731). Excess over basis = capital gain. Reduces your basis in partnership interest.
  

  

  

  
20

  
Other information - attach statement

  

  
   Includes recapture items, unearned income, look-through rules, Section 751 hot assets, and other items requiring partner-level computation. See Schedule K-1 instructions.
  

  

  

  

  
  

  21   More than one activity for at-risk purposes: 
   Check if the partnership has more than one activity subject to the at-risk rules of IRC \'a7465. Requires separate determination of at-risk amounts for each activity.
  22   More than one activity for passive activity purposes: 
   Check if the partnership conducts more than one passive activity. Partners must apply the passive activity loss rules of IRC \'a7469 separately to each activity. Regroup activities if applicable.
  

  

  23   Partner's basis information - attach statement:
   Enter partner's beginning and ending outside basis in the partnership interest. Basis = capital account + share of liabilities. Required to determine if losses are deductible (IRC \'a7704(d)) and to compute gain/loss on disposition of partnership interest (IRC \'a7741).
  

  
Beginning basis: 

  
Ending basis: 

  

  

  
  

  IRC \'a7702: Each partner must take into account separately their distributive share of the partnership's items of income, gain, loss, deduction, or credit.
  IRC \'a7704: Partner's distributive share is determined by the partnership agreement.
  IRC \'a7705: Partner's basis is adjusted annually by income, losses, and distributions.
  IRC \'a7731: No gain/loss recognized on distributions unless cash exceeds outside basis.
  See the Partner's Instructions for Schedule K-1 (Form 1065) for reporting each item on your own return.
  

  
Cat. No. 11394X    Schedule K-1 (Form 1065) (2024)

  

  

  

  
Form 1041-ES

  
Estimated Income Tax for Estates and Trusts \'b7 Installment Vouchers

  

  
  
  

  

  

  
Form 1041-ES

  
Department of the Treasury
Internal Revenue Service

  
OMB No. 1545-0971
20

  

  

  
Estimated Income Tax for Estates and Trusts

  
2024 Estimated Tax Worksheet and Payment Vouchers

  Use this form to figure and pay estimated tax for an estate or trust. See instructions on back.

  

  
2024 Estimated Tax Worksheet

  
1

  

  Adjusted total income expected in 2024 (from Form 1041, line 17)
  
  

  

  
2

  

  Estimated income distribution deduction
  
  

  

  
3

  

  Estimated exemption
  
  

  

  
4

  

  Estimated taxable income (subtract lines 2 and 3 from line 1)
  
  

  

  
5a

  

  Estimated income tax (use 2024 Tax Rate Schedule below)
  
  

  

  
7

  

  Credits (from Form 3800 and other credit forms)
  
  

  

  
9

  

  Total estimated tax (subtract line 7 from line 6)
  
  

  

  
  
Payment Vouchers - Detach and mail with payment

  

  

  
Voucher 1 - Due April 15, 2024

  
Name: 

  EIN: 

  Amount of estimated tax payment: $ 
  

  

  

  
Voucher 2 - Due June 17, 2024

  
Name: 

  EIN: 

  Amount of estimated tax payment: $ 
  

  

  

  
Voucher 3 - Due September 16, 2024

  
Name: 

  EIN: 

  Amount of estimated tax payment: $ 
  

  

  

  
Voucher 4 - Due January 15, 2025

  
Name: 

  EIN: 

  Amount of estimated tax payment: $ 
  

  

  

  
Cat. No. 19734D    Form 1041-ES (2024)

  

  

  

  
Schedule C (Form 1040)

  
Profit or Loss From Business \'b7 Sole Proprietorship \'b7 Accrual Basis \'b7 Auto-populated from Ledger

  

  
  
  
  

  

  

  
SCHEDULE C

  
(Form 1040)
Department of the Treasury
Internal Revenue Service

  
OMB No. 1545-0074
20
Attachment Sequence No. 09

  

  

  
Profit or Loss From Business (Sole Proprietorship)

  
 Go to www.irs.gov/ScheduleC for instructions and the latest information.
 Attach to Form 1040, 1040-SR, 1040-SS, 1040-NR, or 1041; partnerships must file Form 1065.

  

  

  

  A Principal business or profession, including product or service:
  

  C Business name (if no separate business name, leave blank):
  

  E Business address (including suite or room no.):
  
  City, state, ZIP: 
  

  

  B Enter code from instructions: 

  D Employer ID number (EIN): 

  F Accounting method:  (2) Accrual   (1) Cash

  G Did you "materially participate"?  Yes   No

  H If started/acquired during 2024:  Yes

  I Did you make any payments requiring Form 1099?  Yes   No
  

  

  
  
Part I Income

  
1

  

  Gross receipts or sales. See instructions for line 1 and check the box if this income was reported to you on Form W-2 and the "Statutory employee" box on that form was checked
  
  

  

  
2

  

  Returns and allowances
  
  

  

  
3

  

  Subtract line 2 from line 1
  
  

  

  
4

  

  Cost of goods sold (from line 42)
  
  

  

  
5

  

  Other income, including federal and state gasoline or fuel tax credit or refund (see instructions)
  
  

  

  
7

  

  Gross income. Add lines 5 and 6
  
  

  

  
  
Part II Expenses (Enter expenses for business use of your home only on line 30.)

  

  

  
8

  
Advertising

  

  
9

  
Car and truck expenses (Form 4562)

  

  
10

  
Commissions and fees

  

  
11

  
Contract labor (see instructions)

  

  
12

  
Depletion

  

  
13

  
Depreciation and section 179 expense (Form 4562)

  

  
14

  
Employee benefit programs (other than line 19)

  

  
15

  
Insurance (other than health)

  

  
16a

  
Interest - mortgage (paid to banks, etc.)

  

  
16b

  
Interest - other

  

  
17

  
Legal and professional services

  

  

  

  
18

  
Office expense (see instructions)

  

  
19

  
Pension and profit-sharing plans

  

  
20a

  
Rent/lease - vehicles, machinery, equipment

  

  
20b

  
Rent/lease - other business property

  

  
21

  
Repairs and maintenance

  

  
22

  
Supplies (not included in Part III)

  

  
23

  
Taxes and licenses

  

  
24a

  
Travel (see instructions)

  

  
24b

  
Deductible meals (see instructions)

  

  
25

  
Utilities

  

  
26

  
Wages (less employment credits)

  

  
27a

  
Other expenses (from line 48)

  

  
27b

  
Reserved for future use

  

  

  

  
28

  

  Total expenses before expenses for business use of home. Add lines 8 through 27a
  
  

  

  
29

  

  Tentative profit or (loss). Subtract line 28 from line 7
  
  

  

  
30

  

  Expenses for business use of your home. Do not report these expenses elsewhere. Attach Form 8829
  
  

  

  
31

  

  Net profit or (loss). Subtract line 30 from line 29. If a profit, enter on both Schedule 1 (Form 1040), line 3, and on Schedule SE, line 2; also enter on Form 1041, line 3. If a loss, you must go to line 32.
  
  

  

  
  
Part III Cost of Goods Sold (see instructions)

  
33

  

  Method used to value closing inventory:  Cost   Lower of cost or market   Other
  

  

  
35

  

  Inventory at beginning of year (if different from last year's closing inventory, attach explanation)
  
  

  

  
36

  

  Purchases less cost of items withdrawn for personal use
  
  

  

  
42

  

  Cost of goods sold. Subtract line 41 from line 40. Enter the result here and on line 4
  
  

  

  
  
Part V Other Expenses. List below business expenses not included on lines 8\'9626 or line 30.

  

  

  

  Bank Fees and Payment Processing (Stripe, ACH, etc.)
  
  

  

  Subscriptions and Technology Services
  
  

  

  Research and Development
  
  

  

  Solar / Renewable Energy Expenses
  
  

  

  Miscellaneous Operational Costs
  
  

  

  Contractor Training Programs
  
  

  

  Total other expenses - enter on line 27a

EEON ACCOUNTING AGENT - 5 AUTHORIZED AGENTS ACTIVE

  I am your EEON Foundation AI accounting agent. Five authorized agents are active in this hardened, frozen runtime:


  EEON_ACCOUNTING - Primary tax preparation, double-entry accounting, IRS forms, accrual basis, journal entries, ledger sync

  EEON_COMPLIANCE - IRC \'a7501(c)(3) voluntary filing strategy, Glenshaw Glass, IRC \'a7172, \'a738, \'a739

  EEON_DOCUMENT - Document analysis, IRS transcript review, audit defense, evidence preparation

  EEON_NOL_TRACKER - NOL carryforward financial asset tracking, IRC \'a7172(b)(1)(A)(ii) indefinite carryforward

  EEON_REFUND_MANDATE - IRC \'a76402(a) overpayment/refund mandate, IRC \'a76655 estimated payments, Form 1120 Lines 36\'9637, Form 1041 Lines 29c\'9629d


  TWO DIRECTIONS: Journals Forms (Sync from Ledger) \'b7 Forms Journals (Generate Journal Entries)

  FORMS AVAILABLE: 990, 990-EZ, 1120 (full), 1120-W, 1041 (full with Schedule G), 3800 (full Part I/II/III), K-1, 1041-ES, Schedule C Form 1041 Line 3


  Type your question or click to speak.

💰 Bad Debt Refund Calculator — IRC §166

        

          

            

            

            

            
IRC §166 allows a deduction for wholly or partially worthless business or non-business debts. The net bad debt loss reduces taxable income, generating a refund equal to the loss × tax rate.

          

          

            
Results

            

              
Net Bad Debt Loss$0.00

              
Deduction Value$0.00

              
Estimated Refund$0.00

              
Report on Schedule D / Form 8949 or directly on Schedule C (business debts). Use Form 1045 for carryback.

            

          

        

      

    


    
    

      

        
📉 Net Operating Loss Calculator — IRC §172

        

          

            

            

            

            

            

            
IRC §172(b)(1)(A)(ii): NOL carryforwards are indefinite under TCJA (post-2017), limited to 80% of taxable income per year. NOLs are financial assets with dollar-for-dollar monetary value.

          

          

            
NOL Analysis

            

              
Net Operating Loss$0.00

              
80% Limitation / Year$0.00

              
Tax Asset Value$0.00

              
Estimated Refund / Reduction$0.00

              
Report on Form 1120 Line 29a / Form 1040 Schedule A. File Form 1045 for carryback election.

            

          

        

      

    


    
    

      

        
🏦 IRS Interest Refund Calculator — IRC §6611 / §6621

        

          

            

            

            

            

            
IRC §6611: IRS SHALL pay interest on overpayments from the date of overpayment at the §6621 rate (federal short-term rate + 3%). Current rate: 8% (Q4 2024).

          

          

            
Interest Calculation

            

              
Days Outstanding0

              
Daily Interest Rate0.0000%

              
Accrued Interest$0.00

              
Total Refund + Interest$0.00

              
IRS is mandated to pay this interest. Claim by filing Form 843 or noting on your original return. IRC §6402 SHALL refund.

            

          

        

      

    


    
    

      

        
🏛️ Corporate Alternative Minimum Tax (CAMT) — IRC §55 / IIJA §10101

        
Inflation Reduction Act (2022): 15% minimum tax on Adjusted Financial Statement Income (AFSI) for corporations with ≥$1B average 3-year AFSI. Effective tax years beginning after Dec 31, 2022.

        

          

            

            

            

            

            
IRC §55: CAMT = 15% × AFSI, reduced by CAMT foreign tax credit. Applies only if average 3-year AFSI ≥ $1,000,000,000. Additional tax = CAMT − regular tax (if positive). No CAMT carryforward under IRA rules.

          

          

            
CAMT Analysis

            

              
3-Year Average AFSI$0

              
CAMT Threshold Met?—

              
Tentative CAMT (15% × AFSI)$0

              
Regular Tax$0

              
Additional CAMT Owed$0

              

            

          

        

      

    


    
    

      

        
🔄 Stock Buyback Excise Tax — IRC §4501

        
Inflation Reduction Act (2022): 1% excise tax on FMV of stock repurchased by covered corporations. Effective for repurchases after Dec 31, 2022. Reported on Form 720, due by April 30 of the following calendar year.

        

          

            

            

            

              
            

            
IRC §4501(a): 1% excise tax on FMV of any stock repurchased by a covered corporation. Net basis: repurchases minus new stock issued to employees during the year. <10M de minimis threshold per IRC §4501(e). Report on Form 720 by April 30.

          

          

            
Buyback Tax Calculation

            

              
Gross Repurchases$0

              
Employee Stock Issued (Offset)($0)

              
Net Repurchase Base$0

              
De Minimis Exempt? (<$10M)—

              
Excise Tax Due (1%)$0

              
Report on Form 720 by April 30 of following calendar year. Not deductible as a business expense per IRC §4501(f).
